w:r.w:t: The Kindora backend is a well-structured NestJS monolith with a clear domain-driven layout, strong use of cross-cutting interceptors, and a consistent decorator vocabulary. The auth module in particular shows careful thinking around security and cohesion. The overall architecture scores well on modularity and readability.

Three issues need attention before the next sprint: bcrypt cost-factor drift between auth.service and users.service (left over from Phase A), a shadow changePassword method in users.service that bypasses all session-revocation logic, and two dead files. Beyond those, the highest-impact quality improvement is making ListFilterService a proper injectable — it is currently instantiated with new in 16 service methods.
These patterns are working correctly and should be preserved and extended consistently to new modules.
Domain-driven NestJS layout — each feature owns its controller, service, DTOs, entities, serializers, and messages. Cross-module calls go through the public service, not direct repository access.
Every module has messages/error.ts and messages/success.ts. No hardcoded strings in controllers or services (with a few exceptions noted). Makes copy changes and future i18n straightforward.

BaseSerializer and per-entity serializers use @Expose() / @Exclude() from class-transformer. Sensitive fields (password, emailVerificationKey, pkid) are excluded by default. Prevents accidental field leakage at the type level.
ResponseInterceptor: uniform API envelope. AuditLogInterceptor: declarative audit via @LogActivity(). ErrorInterceptor: centralised logging. None of these concerns pollute service code.

JwtAuthGuard -> SchoolContextGuard -> PermissionGuard -> BranchContextGuard chains cleanly and is independently testable. SchoolContextGuard resolves the full permission tree once per request, avoiding repeated DB calls.
@GetUser(), @RequirePermission(), @LogActivity(), @ResponseMessage(), @GetSchoolContext(), @CurrentBranch() eliminate boilerplate. Pattern is consistent across every module.
pkid (FK joins) + uuid id (external exposure) + version + createdAt + updatedAt on every entity. Stable identity scheme across the whole system.
createUser and registerUser use manager.transaction() for atomic User + SchoolMember + SchoolMemberRole writes. No partial state on failure.

auth.service.ts separates login, OTP, password-reset, refresh rotation, session management, and reauth into distinct methods. Private helpers (sha256Hex, hashOTP, signAccessToken, createSession) keep public methods short and focused.
These are bugs or security regressions with measurable impact today.
bcrypt cost factor 10 — three occurrences (registerUser, createUser, changePassword)
Phase A raised auth paths to cost 12 but users.service was not touched. Invited and self-registered users receive weaker hashes.
Shadow changePassword(id, newPassword) — cost 10, no tokenVersion increment, no session revocation
Parallel password-change path that bypasses all security in auth.service. A stolen token can change a password silently.
Dead file: export class Auth {} — empty class, registered nowhere
Stale scaffolding that will confuse future maintainers and may accidentally appear in entity listings.
removeKey duplicated verbatim in both utils files; helper-fns.ts not exported from the barrel
helper-fns.ts is unreachable dead code. Causes confusion about which file to extend.
Phase A raised the cost factor to 12 in auth.service.ts. Three paths in users.service.ts were missed:
All three should be updated to genSaltSync(12). This is a 15-minute fix.

users.service.ts has a public changePassword(id, newPassword) method that hashes with cost 10, does not increment tokenVersion, and does not revoke sessions. The correct implementation lives in auth.service.ts. The users.service version should be deleted; any remaining callers routed through auth.service.
Code quality issues that will compound as the codebase grows. None are regressions today but all will cause problems at scale.
generateRandomPassword uses Math.random() — not cryptographically secure
Placeholder invite passwords are weak. Should use randomBytes() from node:crypto.
ListFilterService is not @Injectable() — instantiated with new in 16 service methods
Cannot be mocked in unit tests. New instance allocated on every paginated query. Cannot be swapped via DI.
Typed as any throughout (repository: any, serializer: any, [key:string]: any)
Suppresses TypeScripts ability to catch misuse at compile time. Conflicts with the noImplicitAny goal.
Blanket eslint-disable at file tops suppress entire rule categories
Hides real type errors. Should be targeted at the offending line, not the whole file.
FORGOT_PASSOWRD_EMAIL_SENT — transposed letters, propagated to controller import
Breaks grep-ability and creates confusion when searching the codebase.
Metadata key is a magic string responseMessage — no named constant
Inconsistent with LOG_ACTIVITY_KEY / CATCH_ERROR_KEY. A typo silently breaks response envelopes.
Mixed import paths — some go directly to the file, others through the barrel
Moving a file breaks direct imports but not barrel imports. Inconsistency increases refactoring cost.
The same pattern appears in 16 service methods across the codebase:
// current — new instance per method call
Add @Injectable(), make the class generic, export from CommonModule, and inject it:
Polish items that improve long-term quality without urgency.
Hardcoded string Branch not found in create() — should be a named constant like BRANCH_NOT_FOUND.
TrimBodyPipe mutates its input object in-place. Return a new object for a pure, side-effect-free transform.
orderBy defaults to pkid — an internal column name leaking to the API surface. Default to createdAt.
require-reauth.decorator.ts exists but is not re-exported from the decorators barrel. Dead export.
Only UsersController has @ApiTags(). Other controllers lack it, making Swagger navigation hard.
isDateAfter and isDateInPast wrap date-fns identically with no added logic. Import date-fns directly.
Ordered by priority. Must Fix items should be done before the next deployment — together they take under an hour.
Replace genSaltSync(10) with genSaltSync(12) in registerUser, createUser, and changePassword in users.service.ts.
Delete users.service changePassword(id, newPassword). Route any callers to auth.service.changePassword.
Delete auth/entities/auth.entity.ts and utils/helper-fns.ts. Confirm no imports reference them.
Rewrite generateRandomPassword to use crypto.randomBytes / crypto.randomInt per character position.
Add @Injectable() and export from CommonModule. Make class generic. Replace 16 new ListFilterService(...) call sites with injected instance.
Replace file-top eslint-disable blocks with targeted eslint-disable-next-line at offending lines, or fix the underlying type issue.
Rename FORGOT_PASSOWRD_EMAIL_SENT. Extract responseMessage string to RESPONSE_MESSAGE_KEY constant.
Replace direct-file imports with barrel imports throughout (src/common/decorators, src/auth/guards, etc.).
Total estimated effort: approximately 5 hours for all Must Fix + Should Fix items combined.
Scores after implementing all Must Fix + Should Fix items:
Shadow method removed, dead files deleted, constants named (M2, M3, M4, S5, S6)
